tools.name: twitter_scrape_profile
tools.description:
Use this when you need to look up a Twitter/X user profile by username or URL. Returns structured profile data including bio, follower/following counts, tweet count, verification status, and recent activity.

1. username: the @handle
2. displayName: full name
3. bio: profile description text
4. foll

tools.name: twitter_search_tweets
tools.description:
Use this when you need to find tweets about a topic, brand, event, or keyword. Returns up to 20 recent tweets matching the query with full text, engagement metrics, author info, and timestamps.

1. query: the search term used
2. results: array of tweet objects
3. Each tweet contains: id, text, autho

tools.name: twitter_get_user_tweets
tools.description:
Use this when you need to see what a specific Twitter/X user has been posting recently. Returns their latest tweets with full text, engagement metrics, and timestamps.

1. username: the @handle queried
2. tweets: array of tweet objects with id, text, createdAt, likes, retweets, replies, views, isRet

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: twitter_scrape_profile, twitter_search_tweets · Public sink: twitter_scrape_profile, twitter_search_tweets, twitter_get_user_tweets.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: twitter_scrape_profile, twitter_search_tweets · Public sink: twitter_scrape_profile, twitter_search_tweets, twitter_get_user_tweets

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
